reason: Both answers correctly identify exactly-once delivery as impossible (Two Generals reasoning), correctly reframe as exactly-once processing via at-least-once plus idempotency, and both give the same core technique (idempotency keys/dedup plus atomic commit with side effects) plus note that external side effects still need their own idempotency. Neither has factual errors. Differences are cosmetic: A cites Kafka EOS and calls the pattern transactional inbox; B cites Kafka EOS, Flink, and SQS FIFO, and names it outbox pattern. Neither extra example changes what the asker would actually do next. Equally clear, equally correct, same actionable takeaway.
